title: Pet evacuation carrier readiness
summary: Keep a carrier or secure lead, identification, recent photo, food, water, medicines, waste supplies, vaccination information, and a familiar cloth ready.
content: Keep a carrier or secure lead, identification, recent photo, food, water, medicines, waste supplies, vaccination information, and a familiar cloth ready. Practise calm entry into the carrier and identify shelters or contacts that accept animals. Do not release a frightened animal in an unfamiliar evacuation area.
materials: Notebook or printed card. Context: “Pet evacuation carrier readiness”.
Relevant labels, contacts, and official alerts — for «Pet evacuation carrier readiness»
